title: eulobus crassifolius
intro: Eulobus crassifolius, also known as the mountain mahonia, is a deciduous shrub native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and Canada. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its berries, which contain a compound called berberine, a natural antimicrobial agent with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of the mountain mahonia include its ability to aid digestion, reduce fever, and provide relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the mountain mahonia include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and berries in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
